title: frequent lockups on the varlink API: calls succeed but never finish
body:
/kind bug
**Description**

podman's varlink API often locks up during operation. cockpit-podman's integration tests reproduce this in about half of the test runs. It does a few varlink calls:

```
io.podman.GetVersion {}
io.podman.ListImages {}
io.podman.ListContainers {}
io.podman.InspectImage {"name":"e4db68de4ff27c2adfea0c54bbb73a61a42f5b667c326de4d7d5b19ab71c6a3b"}
io.podman.InspectImage {"name":"b7b28af77ffec6054d13378df4fdf02725830086c7444d9c278af25312aa39b9"}
io.podman.InspectImage {"name":"f32a97de94e13d29835a19851acd6cbc7979d1d50f703725541e44bb89a1ce91"}
io.podman.CreateContainer {"create":{"args":["docker.io/library/busybox:latest","sh","-c","while echo Hello World; do sleep 1; done"],"resources":{},"name":"busybox-with-tty","tty":true,"publish":["6000:5000/TCP","6001:5001/UDP","8001:8001/TCP"],"env":["APPLE=ORANGE","PEAR=BANANA","RHUBARB=STRAWBERRY"],"volume":["/tmp/tmp.77OizXhCAB:/tmp/ro:ro","/tmp/tmp.YsCaBDzNVq:/tmp/rw:rw"]}}
io.podman.StartContainer {"name":"3193ebd9ddc1c4ea219a09a5a450bbd42e4e5589082e76c03cb8915b285587d8"}
io.podman.GetContainer {"id":"3193ebd9ddc1c4ea219a09a5a450bbd42e4e5589082e76c03cb8915b285587d8"}
io.podman.GetContainer {"id":"3193ebd9ddc1c4ea219a09a5a450bbd42e4e5589082e76c03cb8915b285587d8"}
```

and then podman is completely frozen. `podman ps` hangs forever. Other commands like `podman images` still work, though.

https://209.132.184.41:8493/logs/pull-148-20190718-161717-1d266659-cockpit-fedora-30/ is such a failed test run. 

The journal has literally thousands of these messages:
```
podman[7195]: time="2019-07-18T12:24:11-04:00" level=error msg="Unable to decode event: unknown event type \"\""
```
I don't know where these are coming from yet.

I tried to reproduce this on the command line, but there I hit another bug (I'll file this separately).

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.4.5-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.6
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.6
  podman version: 1.4.5-dev
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.9.0
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.4.4-1.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.2.0, commit: 41010e63c287618b1dc34ee11d10d268e2feeefe'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "30"
  MemFree: 512851968
  MemTotal: 1030520832
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-93.dev.gitb9b6cc6.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc8+dev
      commit: e3b4c1108f7d1bf0d09ab612ea09927d9b59b4e3
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 1291841536
  SwapTotal: 1291841536
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 1
  hostname: m1.cockpit.lan
  kernel: 5.1.16-300.fc30.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 9m 34.51s
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mountopt=nodev,metacopy=on
  GraphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  ImageStore:
    number: 3
  RunRoot: /var/run/containers/storage
  VolumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

This is a standard virt-install Fedora 30 in QEMU.

comments.body:
Do you have anything else running in systemd that might be logging with the unit name "podman"? That sounds like a likely cause.

@baude This sounds like we might be repeatedly parsing old logs in journald - is there a way to stop that?
the lock ups and the warnings are two different things I think.  I cannot be sure, but that is what I would suspect.
Concur. We have another issue regarding deadlocks with Varlink - my suspicion is these two are directly related.

With this loop, I can lock up podman pretty reliably (as root), after a handful of iterations:
```
podman pull docker.io/library/busybox:latest
systemctl start io.podman.socket

while podman rm -f c1; varlink call unix:/run/podman/io.podman/io.podman.CreateContainer '{"create":{"args":["docker.io/library/busybox:latest","sh","-c","while echo Hello World; do sleep 1; done"],"resources":{},"name":"c1","tty":true}}'; varlink call unix:/run/podman/io.podman/io.podman.StartContainer '{"name":"c1"}'; do :; done
```

The journal shows that this is not directly a case of issue #3603 (start limit hit due to too aggressive timeout). Although there *are* two restarts (which demonstrate that even the 1s timeout doesn't work, it's by and large immediate), the lockup happens when the service  keeps running. There is no distinct error message:
```

Jul 19 03:27:16.442715 m1.cockpit.lan podman[8854]: 2019-07-19 03:27:16.442203131 -0400 EDT m=+3.171625282 container init bb75269735d6d2ac2434b37b9a1208c2bbe3889444d97ff9feab8c1e3c181628 (image=docker.io/library/busybox:latest, name=c1)
Jul 19 03:27:16.486319 m1.cockpit.lan podman[8854]: 2019-07-19 03:27:16.486253978 -0400 EDT m=+3.215676117 container start bb75269735d6d2ac2434b37b9a1208c2bbe3889444d97ff9feab8c1e3c181628 (image=docker.io/library/busybox:latest, name=c1)
```
Just the `varlink` process hangs forever:
```
root      9110  0.0  0.0  35272   788 pts/0    S+   03:27   0:00 varlink call unix:/run/podman/io.podman/io.podman.StartContainer {"name":"c1"}
```

Even though the container got started:
```
# podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                             COMMAND               CREATED             STATUS                 PORTS  NAMES
bb75269735d6  docker.io/library/busybox:latest  sh -c while echo ...  About a minute ago  Up About a minute ago         c1
```

and while the `varlink` process above hangs around, the `io.podman.service` keeps running and never times out (which is right, given that there's still a connection).

So maybe the "Unable to decode event" spew is unrelated, it may be issue #3526? Let's concentrate on this lock-up then, which at least on the command line is real (it's not quite a proof that it's the same lockup that we see in the UI, but we can re-test afterwards).

> while the varlink process above hangs around, the io.podman.service keeps running and never times out (which is right, given that there's still a connection).

.. however, after ^C'ing the varlink process, the io.podman.service now keeps running and *never* times out. Supposedly in its brain it was missing the closed connection of the client and still thinks it's connected?

> Do you have anything else running in systemd that might be logging with the unit name "podman"? 

I reproduced the hang again with our cockpit-podman tests. In this case, io.podman.service did not get restarted a single time (i. e. not related to timeouts), and the operation just timed out. In the journal I again see thousands of

    podman[6580]: time="2019-07-19T03:57:50-04:00" level=error msg="Unable to decode event: unknown event type \"\""

messages. This time I confirmed that process 6580 is really the podman varlink process:

    root      6580  2.4  8.5 894768 86104 ?        Ssl  03:57   0:02 /usr/bin/podman varlink unix:/run/podman/io.podman

This is an strace of the `podman varlink` process while it causes the complete lockup:
[podman.lockup.strace.zip](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/3410088/podman.lockup.strace.zip)
I. e. `podman ps` hangs, API calls hang, etc. This is *not* the same as the CLI reproducer above, but it's the one that gets triggered by the UI (it's enough for me to try and start a container manually, which is the *only* action that this strace covers). I cancelled the strace after a few seconds, as it's just stuck in a polling loop.

@martinpitt I am PTO for another week with really poor internet service.  Maybe if I get a rainy day I will look at this but otherwise once I get back.  There is so much information here to digest; however, one thing I see is that when running the container, we have set tty to true.  That might be the key here in that we tie up varlink with console?  I wonder if we -dt the container, if things work as expected.  That would be a huge clue.
@haraldh based on the descriptions here, do you have an guesses what might be going on?
The console should never hit the varlink process. It never leaves Conmon and the container.
I disagree but lets see where it goes.  There might even be a cleanup problem here too.
I get eternal `varlink call CreateContainer` hangs with `"tty": false` as well.
Imsorry, i should have been more specific.  I mean to detach (-d) things
@martinpitt i think commit 438cbf4c8960db0b59a25a3fb38a778e24a69a40 should have resolved this.  Mind try with latest head and verifying ?
Do you plan to do a new release soon, or do you have some COPR or so with daily RPMs?
Planning on cutting podman-1.6.0 rc1 today.  If everything goes well, we should have a release early next week.
The issue is now resolved and the fixes will be released with the Podman v1.6.0. Thanks a lot for opening the issue, @martinpitt!
